title: School of Eternity — tribulation, patience, and the believer
stanceSummary: Trials are woven into life; the Qur’an and hadith frame patience in obedience and gratitude in ease as the believer’s response. Reflections link classical themes—Zarruq, Imam al-Junayd, and the hadith that the believer’s whole affair is good—to accepting the world’s imperfection without despair. Auto-captions below are cleaned for reading; confirm wording against the audio.
alternateQuestions: Why does Islam say trials are good for the believer?
What does patience mean in Islam—not just waiting?
How should Muslims respond to loss and fear?
What is the hadith about the affair of the believer?
